title: Dockerfile and Compose Validation, Image Tests, Fault Injection, and Quality Gates · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Image Contract Tests
summary: A release builds successfully but fails at runtime on a clean host, ships a root default, and loses data when its dependency stalls. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker image contract tests increment in container release test and fault matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test default user, entrypoint, arguments, files, permissions, labels, health, ports, signals, filesystem mutability, and exit behavior from the built image.
Validate canonical Compose configuration, profiles, variables, images, networks, dependency conditions, implementation support, and changed overrides.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Image Contract Tests · Theory Docker Image Contract Tests
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory image builds and answers one happy-path request but fails read-only execution, slow dependency startup, SIGTERM, missing secrets, and clean-host pull. Design layered tests plus deterministic teardown. Competency probe: Test default user, entrypoint, arguments, files, permissions, labels, health, ports, signals, filesystem mutability, and exit behavior from the built image.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Image Contract Tests contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Docker Image Contract Tests · Theory Docker Image Contract Tests.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for test default user, entrypoint, arguments, files, permissions, labels, health, ports, signals, filesystem mutability, and exit behavior from the built image. For Predict Docker Image Contract Tests · Theory Docker Image Contract Tests, keep Docker Image Contract Tests connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name client, daemon, builder, image, container, host-kernel, storage, network, registry, and supply-chain boundaries; predict state and authority; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, cleanup, and live-environment transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example image builds and answers one happy-path request but fails read-only execution, slow dependency startup, SIGTERM, missing secrets, and clean-host pull. Design layered tests plus deterministic teardown. Competency probe: Test default user, entrypoint, arguments, files, permissions, labels, health, ports, signals, filesystem mutability, and exit behavior from the built image. A defensible solution applies Docker Image Contract Tests, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to container release test and fault matrix.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-testing-validation-quality-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-testing-validation-quality-3
    artifact: container-test-plan.yaml
    decision: worked docker testing validation quality 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      static: dockerfile-and-compose-validation
      image-contract: user-files-entrypoint-health
      integration: dependency-and-fault-injection
      transfer: clean-host-pull-and-run
      negative: readonly-slow-stop-missing-secret
    verify: docker buildx build --check . && docker compose config --quiet
    failure: Reject a successful build as runtime proof, a single happy-path curl, mutable local cache, or tests that depend on undeclared host state.
    repair: Layer structural, image, runtime, integration, fault, clean-host, and supply-chain checks with reproducible fixtures and teardown.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Application unit tests make image contract tests redundant. This fails the Docker Image Contract Tests contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
